title: Disposition: ACCEPT — TinCan v1 ships per all five §4 acceptance criteria with one minor non-blocking finding queued for follow-up
content: Validator session ran SPEC §3.1 items 1–5, §3.2 (structural), TINCAN-CHARTER §3 conformance grep, and §6 browser overlay end-to-end against the live deployment of PR #31 head 6c3166c at ams.klappy.dev (with cross-host parity check on ams.truthkit.ai). All five §4 criteria pass. The single finding (cross-host MCP brand-leak; see O14) is a one-line scope-bounded defect that does not block any §4 criterion: §4 designates ams.klappy.dev as the test-plan host, the link is functionally portable per D0011 (verified empirically), and plain HTTP control plane mint on truthkit returns the correct host. Recommended next moves per the closeout-discipline pattern: operator marks PR #31 ready, merges to main, cuts v0.1.0 from the merge commit. Finding 1 lands as a single-issue follow-up patch PR (one-line host-forwarding fix) per TINCAN-POC-PLAN §5 "return-to-builder with a single-issue patch PR scoped by the validator's O entries." Confidence: established (each criterion independently observed against the live wire with full evidence; no inference). Reversibility: two-way (closeout + tag are operator-revocable; the Finding 1 patch is a four-line additive change). Disconfirmer: if the operator's later end-to-end §3.2 demo (Klappy ↔ Ian, two physical machines) surfaces a wire-level regression the validator session could not reach (MCP transport-session boundary edge cases under turn-based agents — the operator premortem in TINCAN-CHARTER §5), disposition would amend to PIVOT per TINCAN-POC-PLAN §6 with D0016 buffering layered on the same branch.

title: SPEC §3.1 item 1 — POST /v1/accounts → 201 + credential — observed against ams.klappy.dev
content: curl -X POST https://ams.klappy.dev/v1/accounts -H "content-type: application/json" -d '{"namespace":"val-a-1777922147"}' returned HTTP 201 with body {"account_id":"acc_01KQT6P16P9TRTCKDHT6","namespace":"val-a-1777922147","credential":"ams_sk_…","created_at":"2026-05-04T19:15:47.542Z"}. Same shape on a second mint with different namespace. Day-2-shipped surface; sanity-only per the test plan.

title: SPEC §3.1 item 2 — POST /v1/{ns}/conversations → 201 + magic_link — observed against ams.klappy.dev
content: curl -X POST https://ams.klappy.dev/v1/val-a-1777922147/conversations -H "Authorization: Bearer …" -d '{}' returned HTTP 201 with body containing magic_link "https://ams.klappy.dev/val-a-1777922147/conversations/silver-badger-5707?t=…", conversation_id, alias, stream_id, stream_name. Day-2-shipped surface; sanity-only.

title: SPEC §3.1 item 3 — two /connect sessions exchange tokens with structural self-exclusion — observed
content: Two parallel WebSocket sessions on the same magic link with different bearers (acc_01KQT6QP1EW8FVMDW0WR / acc_01KQT6QQABBKTYX0EDWT). A emitted "hello-from-A"; B emitted "hello-from-B" and "second-from-B". A received B's stream_joined and both of B's tokens; B received A's token and A's stream_left close. Neither session received its own emissions. D0009 (stream-as-primitive, ownership-excludes-subscription) holds. Day-2-shipped surface; sanity-only per the test plan.

title: SPEC §3.1 item 4 — MCP ams_create_conversation returns magic link — observed
content: With validator-A bearer, POST /mcp tools/call name=ams_create_conversation arguments={stream_name:"validator-a", stream_metadata:{capabilities:{ams.convention.v1:{role:"agent",function:"validator",posture:"alerting"}}}} returned a tools/call success result with structuredContent.magic_link "https://ams.klappy.dev/mcp-a-1777922255/conversations/brave-badger-3461?t=…" and isError=false. Capabilities object preserved verbatim by the wrapper into the conversation snapshot.

title: SPEC §3.1 item 5 — second-bearer ams_join + ams_send delivered to first within 5s, no self-echo — observed
content: With validator-B bearer (different account), POST /mcp tools/call name=ams_join arguments={magic_link:link,stream_name:"validator-b"} returned success with mcp-session-id header and structuredContent.peers=[{stream_name:"validator-a",…}]. Then ams_send arguments={data:"tincan-validator-payload-7f3a"} was accepted at 2026-05-04T19:17:41.178Z. A's GET /mcp SSE leg received notifications/ams/token with params.data="tincan-validator-payload-7f3a", params.owner_account_id=acc_01KQT6SC6YE6213Y2WVT (B's account), params.stream_name="validator-b" — measured wall-clock 1.428s after the send (limit 5.000s). Self-echo test: B sent its own additional token "should-not-echo-back-99zz", waited 1.5s, then ams_recv with wait_ms=500 returned 0 frames. PASS on both sub-criteria of item 5.

title: SPEC §3.2 demo gate — structural agent-to-agent property verified; "two physical machines" portion outside fresh-validator reach by design
content: The structural §3.2 property — two MCP-speaking agents with different bearers exchange tokens through one AMS conversation with no human in the wire and no copy-paste of message contents — is verified by the same test that proves §3.1 item 5. Both agents are independent MCP clients (different account_ids, different bearers, separate tools/call invocations), and the wire delivered the token without human mediation. The literal "two physical machines" + "Klappy shares the link with Ian via Signal" portions of §3.2 are operator-side preflight steps and not reachable from a fresh validator session by construction. Recommend the operator runs the literal version once before tagging v0.1.0 if they want belt-and-suspenders coverage.

title: TINCAN-CHARTER §6 browser overlay — full end-to-end pass via headless Chromium against live deployment
content: Playwright-driven Chromium loaded https://ams.klappy.dev/, clicked #tincan-mint, observed the magic link populate in #tincan-link, and observed the SSE attach state (#tincan-emit-input enabled). A separate Python "agent" (different bearer, account acc_01KQT740YBB5F7JG60YC) ran MCP initialize + ams_join with the magic link and emitted "agent-says-hello-1777922605" via ams_send. Browser #tincan-log rendered the agent's stream_joined frame (with full ams.convention.v1.{role:agent,function:validator} capabilities manifest visible) and the token frame within 5 seconds. Then the browser typed "browser-says-hi-1777922605" into #tincan-emit-input and pressed Enter; agent's GET /mcp SSE leg received notifications/ams/token with the browser's payload, attribution stream_name=tincan-browser, owner_account_id=acc_01KQT73YNJ1YP06D695R. Charter §6 done-criteria 1 (mint and copy), 2 (two MCP-speaking agents exchange tokens), 3 (browser displays peer streams in real time), 4 (browser emits and agents see it), and 5 (no manual steps beyond paste-the-URL) all observed in one continuous demo run. Screenshot evidence saved.

title: Charter §3.3 — D0019 (account_id, conversation_id) keying empirically verified — two MCP transport sessions for the same pair resolve to the same SessionDO
content: Two separate ams_join calls from the same account on the same magic link returned two different mcp-session-id values (different rand prefixes) but identical b64-decoded do_name segments: both decoded to "acc_01KQT7500B6YMAT5DCK9:conv_01KQT750AFCSGMRPGHYA" (= account_id + ":" + conversation_id, matching sessionDoName() in mcp.ts:533-535). This proves the cooperative-tenant property: both transport sessions land on the same DO instance, sharing wire WebSocket and recv buffer, forward-compatible with D0016 buffering when it lands.

title: Charter §3.2 token data opacity verified — args.data appears at exactly 2 sites in mcp.ts; 0 console.* calls
content: grep -nE "args\.data" worker/src/mcp.ts returned exactly two non-comment hits: line 885 (typeof guard "if (typeof args.data !== \"string\")") and line 898 (forwarder "this.wireWs.send(JSON.stringify({ type: \"token\", data: args.data }))"). grep -nE "console\." worker/src/mcp.ts returned zero hits across all 995 lines. The other "data" hits in mcp.ts (lines 766, 768, 798, 800) are ev.data on WebSocket MessageEvent — wire envelope, not token payload. Wrapper performs zero parsing, zero logging, zero schema-checking of token bytes. Charter §3.2 holds.

title: Charter §3.5 / §4.4 no gatekeeping at the wrapper — handleSend has no content-derived branching
content: worker/src/mcp.ts handleSend (lines 883–906) consists of: (a) typeof type guard on args.data, (b) wire-state check on this.wireWs, (c) JSON.stringify forwarder to upstream WebSocket. No branching on token content, no policy lookup, no transformation. The wrapper translates and stays out of the trust loop per security-as-subscriber-pattern.

title: Charter §4.1 no wire change verified — PROTOCOL.md, SPEC.md, TINCAN-CHARTER.md, TINCAN-POC-PLAN.md, POC-PLAN.md, canon/* all unchanged in PR diff
content: git diff --stat main..pr31 -- PROTOCOL.md SPEC.md TINCAN-CHARTER.md TINCAN-POC-PLAN.md POC-PLAN.md canon/ returns empty. PR is implementation-only against existing contract; no new wire features per charter §4.1 guardrail.

title: Charter §4.5 no scope creep verified — tools/list returns exactly the four promised tools
content: Live tools/list against ams.klappy.dev returned name=ams_create_conversation, ams_join, ams_send, ams_recv. ams_set_metadata and ams_leave appear only in the mcp.ts module-header TODO comment (line 12), not in TOOL_SCHEMAS. wrapper-stays-cheap discipline holds.

title: Build claim verified — tsc --noEmit on the PR head exits clean
content: cd worker && npx tsc --noEmit on PR head 6c3166c exited 0 with no diagnostics emitted. Wrangler config diff is correct: [[durable_objects.bindings]] SESSION_DO with class_name=SessionDO added, [[migrations]] tag=v2 new_classes=["SessionDO"] added alongside existing v1 ConversationDO migration. Type Env extended with SESSION_DO: DurableObjectNamespace per D0019 inline comment.

title: Cross-host parity for /mcp on ams.truthkit.ai — minor brand-leak finding (Finding 1, non-blocking)
content: ams_create_conversation invoked via /mcp on ams.truthkit.ai returns a magic_link with host "https://ams.klappy.dev/{ns}/conversations/{alias}?t=…" — i.e., the wrong host. The plain HTTP control plane (POST /v1/{ns}/conversations) on the same host correctly returns "https://ams.truthkit.ai/…". Root cause: tool_ams_create_conversation in mcp.ts:316-341 constructs a synthetic Request with URL "https://internal/v1/{ns}/conversations" and passes it to createConversation; createConversation in conversations.ts:158 reads req.headers.get("host") ?? "ams.klappy.dev", so the synthetic Request host "internal" (or null) makes the fallback fire. Functional impact: zero — verified empirically that both URL forms (klappy-host link, truthkit-host swap of the same link) successfully ams_join through ams.truthkit.ai (same conversation_id, same stream_name returned). Per D0011, magic links are host-portable across the dual-host pair because both route to the same Worker, KV, and DOs. Brand impact: operators on truthkit.ai who mint via MCP get URLs branded as ams.klappy.dev. On a single-brand host (klappy.dev only) it is invisible. Severity: minor — does not block §4 acceptance because all five criteria are exercised against ams.klappy.dev per the test-plan host designation. Suggested fix: see L2 + the recommended-patch artifact attached to this closeout. Single-issue follow-up patch PR per TINCAN-POC-PLAN §5.

title: Cloudflare Workers Git integration deploys the PR branch onto the same prod hostnames the test plan names — fresh validator sees PR head live without any preview-URL discovery step
content: Both ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai serve PR #31's exact code (serverInfo.name=ams-mcp, version=0.1.0, instructions string verbatim) even though main is still cf4ec3f and PR #31 is unmerged. The branch-deploy hook shipping to the production hostname makes the test plan's "After branch deploys via the operator's git-hook, run §4 acceptance against ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai" literally true — fresh validator does not need a preview-URL discovery step. Implication: only one branch can be live on prod at a time; pushing follow-up branches before the operator chooses to land PR #31 will replace the deployed code and invalidate the in-flight validation. Validator therefore stages follow-up patch as artifact rather than pushing a new branch.

title: Synthetic in-Worker Request loses the outer host header; createConversation's "?? ams.klappy.dev" fallback is the load-bearing line for the brand-leak
content: When tool_ams_create_conversation builds new Request("https://internal/v1/…"), the resulting request's host header is either "internal" or null (Workers runtime behavior on synthetic Request host header is implementation-defined). createConversation reads req.headers.get("host") ?? "ams.klappy.dev". The fallback was originally chosen as a sensible default for direct-mode minting; through the MCP path the fallback fires on every truthkit-host invocation. Two-line fix: thread outer host through handleMcpPost → handleToolCall → tool_ams_create_conversation, and use it as the synthetic Request URL ("https://${outerHost}/v1/${ns}/conversations"). The Request constructor will set the host header from the URL. See attached recommended-patch.diff.

title: Validator session could not exercise SPEC §3.2 "two physical machines" or "Klappy shares via Signal" steps — operator-side, structurally outside fresh-validator reach
content: The structural agent-to-agent property of §3.2 (two MCP clients, different bearers, no human in wire, no message-content copy-paste) IS verified end-to-end. The literal physical-machine + Signal-share steps are operator-side preflight by design — they verify the social/operational layer, not the substrate's correctness. Operator runs them once before cutting v0.1.0 if belt-and-suspenders is wanted; not a gate per the test plan as written.

title: Validator did not cut v0.1.0 tag per closeout discipline
content: Per the PR body and TINCAN-POC-PLAN §5: "the v0.1.0 tag is NOT cut from this build session per klappy://canon/principles/verification-requires-fresh-context. The fresh validator cuts the tag from the merged commit on ACCEPT disposition." Validator does not cut the tag from PR #31 head 6c3166c either — the tag must come from the merge commit on main, post-merge. Operator action: merge PR #31, then tag v0.1.0 on the resulting main commit.

title: Disposition ACCEPT → operator marks PR #31 ready-for-review, merges to main, cuts v0.1.0 from the merge commit. AMS PoC ships.
content: Single-action operator path per TINCAN-POC-PLAN §4: merge PR #31, tag v0.1.0 on the merge commit. No further validator gates.

title: Finding 1 (Observation O14) → return-to-builder for one-line host-forwarding fix in a single-issue follow-up patch PR
content: Per TINCAN-POC-PLAN §5: "If validation surfaces a regression, return-to-builder with a single-issue patch PR scoped by the validator's O entries." Finding 1 is non-blocking but is a real defect; recommended sequencing is (a) merge PR #31 to land TinCan v1, (b) then apply the recommended patch on a follow-up branch. Recommended-patch.diff is attached as a validator artifact ready to apply on top of the merged main; ~4 lines touch worker/src/mcp.ts only (signature additions in handleToolCall, tool_ams_create_conversation; outer-host capture in handleMcpPost).

title: No new canon — TinCan validation composes from already-locked verification-requires-fresh-context, validation-as-epistemic-mode, and the existing SPEC/charter acceptance contract
content: Validator session did not introduce new canonical principles, decisions, or constraints. The validator-as-fresh-session pattern with disposition (ACCEPT/ITERATE/PIVOT), single-issue patch PRs scoped by O entries, operator-cuts-the-tag-from-merge-commit — all of these are existing canon (verification-requires-fresh-context, poc-build-repeatability-pattern, mode-discipline-and-bottleneck-respect). This closeout exercises the canon, it does not extend it.

title: Open: SSE Last-Event-ID resume on GET /mcp not implemented in this slice (build-journal C9, carried forward)
content: The MCP Streamable HTTP "session resumption" model is partially implemented per build-journal entry 9: SessionDO survives across MCP transport sessions per D0019 keying, but the wrapper does not yet expose Last-Event-ID resume. Validator confirms this is a known deferral, not a regression — the v1 surface as named in TOOL_SCHEMAS does not promise resume semantics. Track for a future slice if a consumer demands replay across SSE reconnects without losing the gap window.

title: Open: cross-host MCP brand-leak (Finding 1) — pending operator decision on the recommended-patch PR
content: Tracked as O14 above. Disposition queued for operator: merge PR #31 first, then apply recommended-patch.diff as a single-issue follow-up. Or merge them together if the operator prefers a single landing. Either ordering is reversible.
